[Planner]
1. System Overview
- Purpose: A backend service that accepts PDF, TXT, and Markdown uploads, validates and stores originals/metadata, extracts text, segments content into searchable units, builds a search index, and supports asynchronous processing with retryable steps and deduplication. It should accommodate adding new formats with minimal changes and be maintainable for a small team.
- Non-functional goals: scalability within a small team, maintainability, observability (logs, metrics, traces), eventual consistency for indexing, and robust error handling with idempotent operations where feasible.
- Boundaries: No front-end UI in scope; all interactions via REST APIs and asynchronous task processing. No reliance on external proprietary services beyond standard open tooling.

2. Main Components and Responsibilities
- API Gateway / REST API Layer
  - Input: file upload requests, metadata, search queries.
  - Output: processing status, search results, API errors.
- Ingestion Service
  - Input: upload request, file stream, metadata.
  - Output: stored original, metadata record, deduped record, task enqueue events.
- Deduplication & Metadata Store
  - Input: file content hash, metadata fields (user, filename, size).
  - Output: deduplicated document ID, idempotent upload handling.
- Storage Layer
  - Original Store: blob/object store for uploaded files (e.g., S3-like).
  - Metadata Store: lightweight DB for document records (document_id, status, hashes, timestamps, user info).
  - Text/Index Store: extracted text segments, indices, and search metadata.
- Text Extraction & Processing Engine (Worker/Task System)
  - Input: document_id, file location, detected format.
  - Output: extracted full text, segmenting into searchable sections, per-segment metadata.
  - Features: format-specific extractors, pluggable via registry; streaming/incremental extraction where supported.
- Indexing Service
  - Input: document segments with metadata.
  - Output: search index entries; per-document index status.
- Search Service
  - Input: user query, optional filters.
  - Output: ranked results with snippet fragments and document references.
- Task/Queue System (or Orchestrator)
  - Input: work items per document (extract, index, reprocess on failure).
  - Output: status updates, retry signals, backoff control.
- Status & Monitoring
  - Aggregates: current processing status, per-document lifecycle, retry counts, error reasons.
  - Outputs: metrics, logs, traces for observability.

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Upload
  - Client uploads a file and metadata via POST /documents with content-type validation (application/pdf, text/plain, text/markdown) and size limit checks.
  - Ingestion Service computes a content hash (e.g., SHA-256) and checks Deduplication Store.
  - If a duplicate exists (same hash and minimal metadata override), return existing document_id with current status; do not create new records.
  - Otherwise persist original file to Original Store, create Metadata record with status "QUEUED", enqueue Extract-and-Index task chain.
- Extraction & Segmentation
  - Worker retrieves document, selects format-specific extractor (PDF, TXT, MD).
  - Extract text; validate extraction success; split into sections (e.g., chapters/paragraphs or fixed-size chunks with offsets).
  - Persist extracted text segments and per-segment metadata to Text/Index Store.
  - Enqueue indexing task per document.
- Indexing
  - Indexer ingests segments; builds or updates a full-text index (document-level and segment-level). 
  - Update document status to "INDEXED" when complete; on partial failures, mark for retry.
- Search
  - Client queries via GET /search?q=...; Search Service queries the index, returns ranked results with snippets and document references.
- Status Reporting
  - Clients can poll GET /documents/{id}/status to observe pipeline state: UPLOADED, EXTRACTING, INDEXING, INDEXED, FAILED, or RETRYING with retry counts and error messages.
- Retries and Failures
  - Failures at any step trigger retries with exponential backoff up to configurable max attempts.
  - If maximum retries exhausted, status becomes FAILED with diagnostic details; optional manual retry endpoint.

4. REST API Design
- Endpoints (high-level)
- POST /documents
  - Purpose: upload a new document; initiate processing.
  - Main Request Data: multipart/form-data or JSON with file binary, filename, size, user_id, optional tags.
  - Main Response Data: { document_id, status: "QUEUED" | "PROCESSING" | "INDEXING" | "INDEXED" | "FAILED", message, retry_after_ms? }
- GET /documents/{id}/status
  - Purpose: report current lifecycle status and last error (if any).
  - Main Response Data: { document_id, status, last_error, retry_count, timestamps }
- GET /documents/{id}
  - Purpose: retrieve document metadata and status; optionally include index metadata.
  - Main Response Data: { document_id, hash, filename, size, format, status, created_at, updated_at, index_status }
- GET /search
  - Purpose: perform a full-text search over indexed documents.
  - Main Request Data: query string, optional filters (tags, date range, document_ids), pagination (page, page_size)
  - Main Response Data: { total, page, page_size, results: [{ document_id, score, snippet, title }] }
- POST /documents/{id}/retry
  - Purpose: manually trigger a retry for failed steps (idempotent guard).
  - Main Response Data: { document_id, status, next_retry_after_ms }

5. Data Model
- Document
  - document_id (UUID)
  - user_id (string)
  - filename (string)
  - size (int)
  - format (enum: PDF, TXT, MD, OTHER)
  - hash (string)
  - status (enum)
  - created_at, updated_at
  - retry_count (int)
  - last_error (string, nullable)
- Segment
  - segment_id (UUID)
  - document_id (FK)
  - text (large)
  - start_offset (int)
  - end_offset (int)
  - metadata (JSON) // e.g., section_id, title_hint
- IndexEntry
  - index_id (UUID)
  - document_id (FK)
  - segment_id (FK, nullable)
  - text (for quick snippets or stored field)
  - vector (not required in this design unless embeddings are added)
  - rank_factors (JSON)
- Audit/Meta
  - event_id
  - document_id
  - event_type
  - payload (JSON)
  - created_at

6. Suggested Project Structure
- src/
  - api/
    - routes.go (HTTP handlers)
    - validators.go
  - services/
    - ingestion.go
    - dedup.go
    - extraction.go (format registry)
    - indexing.go
    - search.go
    - status.go
  - workers/
    - extractor_worker.go
    - indexer_worker.go
  - storage/
    - blob_store.go
    - metadata_store.go
    - index_store.go
  - formats/
    - interfaces.go
    - pdf_extractor.go
    - txt_extractor.go
    - md_extractor.go
  - queue/
    - scheduler.go
    - backoff.go
  - models/
    - document.go
    - segment.go
    - index_entry.go
  - config/
    - config.go
  - tests/
- infra/
  - docker/
  - k8s/
  - monitoring/
  - logging/
- cmd/
  - api_server/main.go

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Duplicate prevention
  - Compute content hash on upload; check duplicates in Deduplication Store by hash + user scope.
  - If identical hash exists and metadata is compatible, return existing document_id with current status; else create new record but reuse the same hash for future dedup checks.
- Retry strategy
  - Exponential backoff with jitter; max attempts per step (configurable).
  - Idempotent operations for retries: ensure re-processing does not corrupt state (use document_id as the unique arbiter).
- Error handling
  - Structured errors with codes (e.g., ERR_INVALID_FORMAT, ERR_TOO_LARGE, ERR_EXTRACTION_FAILED, ERR_INDEXING_FAILED).
  - On non-recoverable errors, mark document status as FAILED and surface error details to status endpoint.
- Extensibility
  - Format registry for extractors: each format implements a common interface (initialize, extract, segment).
  - New formats registered without core flow changes.

8. Testing Plan
- Unit tests
  - Hashing and dedup logic
  - Extractors for each format with sample inputs
  - Segmenting logic correctness
  - Indexing data flow and search response correctness
- Integration tests
  - Upload flow including duplicate detection
  - End-to-end happy path: upload -> extract -> index -> search
  - Failure scenarios: corrupt file, extraction failure, indexing failure, retry behavior
- Performance tests
  - Large PDFs: verify streaming extraction paths and chunking
  - Concurrent uploads: basic concurrency tests to ensure idempotent dedupe and queue handling
- Observability tests
  - Logs contain trace ids; metrics emitted for processing stages; alertable thresholds

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Strong vs eventual consistency in indexing
  - Choice: Eventual consistency for indexing to simplify asynchronous processing and improve throughput with the small team.
  - Implication: Short-term search results may lag after uploads; clients may see initial indexing latency. This aligns with the requirement of asynchronous processing and retryable steps, while enabling simpler maintainability. If strict immediacy is needed, a synchronous index build path could be added for small documents, but at the cost of tighter coupling and reduced throughput.

Evidence, Assumptions, and Unresolved Issues (to Manager)

  - Object storage and a relational or NoSQL metadata store are available and accessible.
  - A pluggable extractor registry will be used to facilitate new formats without core changes.
  - eventual consistency in indexing is acceptable for user-facing search latency.

1. System Overview
The service enables users to upload documents (PDF, TXT, Markdown), stores originals and metadata, extracts text, segments into searchable units, builds an index, enables document search, and reports processing status. The system emphasizes maintainability for a small development team, clear failure modes, observability, and straightforward testing.

2. Main Components and Responsibilities
Component table
- Component: Ingestion API Gateway
  Main Responsibility: Accepts file uploads; validates type/size; initiates processing workflow; returns status/links.
  Input: Upload request with file, metadata, user id.
  Output: Acknowledgement response with job/record id; initial status.
- Component: Event/Task Queue
  Main Responsibility: Orchestrates asynchronous processing; manages retries and backoff; guarantees at-least-once delivery to workers.
  Input: Processing tasks from ingestion; worker results; retry signals.
  Output: Task messages to workers; status updates to workflow store.
- Component: Document Store
  Main Responsibility: Persistes original files and metadata; supports dedup checks; stores per-document state.
  Input: File binary, metadata, computed hash.
  Output: Storage references (bucket path, metadata record id).
- Component: Metadata & State Store
  Main Responsibility: Persistes processing state, batch information, and index mappings; provides idempotent dedup checks.
  Input: Document hash, metadata, processing status updates.
  Output: State records; dedup outcomes.
- Component: Text Extraction Engine (Format Handlers)
  Main Responsibility: Extracts text from documents; supports incremental streaming for large files; provides per-format extraction results.
  Input: Document reference; raw file.
  Output: Extracted text, language, token boundaries, and per-section metadata.
- Component: Sectionizer / Segmenter
  Main Responsibility: Divides extracted text into searchable sections (e.g., sections, paragraphs, or fixed-size chunks); emits section records.
  Input: Extracted text; document metadata.
  Output: Section objects with offsets.
- Component: Indexer
  Main Responsibility: Builds and maintains the search index for sections; supports updates to reflect new or updated documents.
  Input: Section data; document metadata; dedup identifiers.
  Output: Index segments/entries; index health/status.
- Component: Search API
  Main Responsibility: Executes user queries against the index; returns matching sections and document context.
  Input: Query string, filters, pagination.
  Output: Search results with document references and snippets.
- Component: Status & Observability
  Main Responsibility: Provides progress/status endpoints, metrics, logs, traces; centralizes error reporting.
  Input: Processing events, health checks.
  Output: Status dashboards, alerting hooks.
- Component: Plugin Registry (Extensibility Layer)
  Main Responsibility: Enables easy addition of new document formats via format-specific handlers; registers handlers and their metadata.
  Input: New handler definitions; format IDs.
  Output: Accessible handler registry for Ingestion and Extraction components.

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Upload: User posts a file (PDF/TXT/Markdown) with metadata. Ingestion API validates type/size, computes a content hash, checks for existing dedup records, stores the original file and metadata, and creates a processing job with a unique document-id.
- Deduplication: If a document with the same content hash already exists and is fully indexed, return a duplicate-notified response; otherwise proceed with processing (idempotent gate via hash).
- Extraction: A worker consumes the job, selects the appropriate Format Handler via the Plugin Registry, and extracts text (with streaming support for large files as needed). Emits extracted_text and per-section data.
- Segmentation: Sectionizer divides the text into searchable units (paragraphs or fixed-size chunks) and emits Section records with offsets and document linkage.
- Indexing: Indexer builds/updates the search index with the new sections; supports incremental indexing and atomic commit to ensure consistency.
- Status Update: Processing status is updated at each stage (Pending → Extracted → Segmented → Indexed → Complete). Any failure triggers a retry with backoff; critical steps are idempotent to allow safe replays.
- Search: Clients query the Search API to retrieve matching sections and document context, with optional filters and pagination.
- Reporting: Users receive processing status updates and, upon completion, can perform searches against the index. Logs and metrics are collected in the Status & Observability component.

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Deduplication
  - Compute content_hash from file content; check existing Document by hash in Metadata store.
  - If exists and status=Complete, respond with a “already_uploaded” status; do not re-index.
  - If exists but incomplete, requeue or join an ongoing processing pipeline (idempotent join).
- Retries and Backoff
  - Processing steps are retried with exponential backoff and jitter; cap at a maximum delay.
  - Idempotent operations: extraction, segmentation, and indexing are designed to be replay-safe; repeated messages do not create duplicate sections or index entries.
- Error Handling
  - Transient errors (network, transient IO) trigger retries.
  - Permanent errors (unsupported format, corrupt file) mark processing_status=Failed with reason.
  - Centralized error registry to surface to Status & Observability.
- Duplicate Prevention
  - Content-based dedup is primary; metadata-based dedup as secondary (e.g., exact filename and user with identical hash could be treated as same document or versioned if versioning is needed).
- Observability
  - Structured logs with document_id, job_id, stage, and error codes.
  - Metrics: processing_times, queue depth, success/failure rates, indexing throughput.
- Concurrency
  - Processing jobs are serialized per document to avoid concurrent conflicting writes; global workers scale horizontally.

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Immediate indexing vs. eventual consistency
  - Choice: Indexing is performed asynchronously after extraction and segmentation. This favors throughput and responsiveness of ingestion endpoints and allows scalable write workloads. However, it introduces eventual consistency for search results; new documents may not appear in search instantly. This design favors maintainability and simplicity for a small team, while still delivering timely search updates. If stronger consistency is required, the system could switch to synchronous indexing with stricter transactional guarantees, at the cost of higher latency and reduced throughput.

  - The indexer supports incremental updates; reindexing a document replaces stale sections.

  - Observability instrumentation specifics (which tracing system, metrics library) need finalization.

  - Indexing Service ingests sections into the search index; on success, mark document as READY in state store.
  - Any transient failure triggers a retry with exponential backoff; non-recoverable failures transition to FAILED with logs.

- Search:
  - Client queries /api/search with keywords and optional filters.
  - Search Service queries the index, returns ranked results with snippet and document references, including processing status if relevant.

- Status reporting:

Key design ideas:
- Separate concerns via micro-service style boundaries, but within a monorepo for maintainability.
- Use a central job/queue (e.g., Redis-backed) to coordinate asynchronous steps with backoff.
- Format handlers registered in a registry; default handlers for PDF, TXT, Markdown; add new handlers by implementing a defined interface and registering them.

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention

- Validation errors (invalid type, oversized file) result in immediate 4xx with actionable messages; no side effects.
- Transient failures (network, IO, temporary unavailability) trigger exponential backoff retries up to a configurable max (e.g., 7 attempts over hours).
- Idempotency keys: on upload, compute content hash; if a matching hash and uploaderId exist, return existing documentId and status to prevent duplicates. If the hash exists but the document is CANCELLED/FAILED, allow re-upload to create a new record while preserving history.
- Retries of specific steps (extraction, indexing) are isolated per job; failures do not cascade to unrelated jobs.
- Partial processing: if extraction succeeds but indexing fails, document remains PROCESSING/INDEXING with a retry path to complete indexing; partial indices can be reconciled via idempotent index updates.

- Unit tests for:
  - Hash dedup logic and metadata association

  - Retry/backoff calculations
- Integration tests:
  - End-to-end upload -> processing -> search path with sample PDFs, TXT, Markdown
  - Duplicate upload prevention path
  - Failure-mode tests (forcing extraction/indexing failures and ensuring backoff/retry)
- Performance tests (sampling small documents; plan for larger documents as future work)
- Observability tests:
  - Verify status transitions and metrics emission (latency, throughput, error rate)
  - Trace flow across services for a single job

9. Design Tradeoff

- Tradeoff: Centralized queue vs fully distributed orchestrator
  - A centralized, pluggable queue simplifies retries, backoff, and idempotency, reducing the likelihood of duplicate work in a small team setting. It also provides clear checkpoints for observability. However, it adds a single point of contention and potential bottlenecks if throughput scales drastically. To mitigate, the design supports horizontal scaling of the queue system and stateless workers, enabling easier growth and maintainability while preserving deterministic retry semantics.

- Concurrency controls for concurrent uploads by the same user and how to surface user-visible progress in real-time (potential need for websockets/push events).
- Compliance and retention policies for metadata and documents are pending legal review and may affect storage lifecycle.

  - If a duplicate exists (same hash and minimal metadata override), return existing document_id with current status; do not create new records.
  - Otherwise persist original file to Original Store, create Metadata record with status "QUEUED", enqueue Extract-and-Index task chain.
- Extraction & Segmentation
  - Worker retrieves document, selects format-specific extractor (PDF, TXT, MD).
  - Extract text; validate extraction success; split into sections (e.g., chapters/paragraphs or fixed-size chunks with offsets).

  - Indexer ingests segments; builds or updates a full-text index (document-level and segment-level).

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Upload: User posts a file (PDF/TXT/Markdown) with metadata. Ingestion API validates type/size, computes a content hash, checks for existing dedup records, stores the original file and metadata, and creates a processing job with a unique document-id.
- Deduplication: If a document with the same content hash already exists and is fully indexed, return a duplicate-notified response; otherwise proceed with processing (idempotent gate via hash).
- Extraction: A worker consumes the job, selects the appropriate Format Handler via the Plugin Registry, and extracts text (with streaming support for large files as needed). Emits extracted_text and per-section data.
- Segmentation: Sectionizer divides the text into searchable units (paragraphs or fixed-size chunks) and emits Section records with offsets and document linkage.
- Indexing: Indexer builds/updates the search index with the new sections; supports incremental indexing and atomic commit to ensure consistency.
- Status Update: Processing status is updated at each stage (Pending → Extracted → Segmented → Indexed → Complete). Any failure triggers a retry with backoff; critical steps are idempotent to allow safe replays.
- Search: Clients query the Search API to retrieve matching sections and document context, with optional filters and pagination.
- Reporting: Users receive processing status updates and, upon completion, can perform searches against the index. Logs and metrics are collected in the Status & Observability component.

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Deduplication
  - Compute content_hash from file content; check existing Document by hash in Metadata store.
  - If exists and status=Complete, respond with a “already_uploaded” status; do not re-index.
  - If exists but incomplete, requeue or join an ongoing processing pipeline (idempotent join).
- Retries and Backoff
  - Processing steps are retried with exponential backoff and jitter; cap at a maximum delay.
  - Idempotent operations: extraction, segmentation, and indexing are designed to be replay-safe; repeated messages do not create duplicate sections or index entries.
- Error Handling
  - Transient errors (network, transient IO) trigger retries.
  - Permanent errors (unsupported format, corrupt file) mark processing_status=Failed with reason.
  - Centralized error registry to surface to Status & Observability.
- Duplicate Prevention
  - Content-based dedup is primary; metadata-based dedup as secondary (e.g., exact filename and user with identical hash could be treated as same document or versioned if versioning is needed).
- Observability
  - Structured logs with document_id, job_id, stage, and error codes.
  - Metrics: processing_times, queue depth, success/failure rates, indexing throughput.
- Concurrency
  - Processing jobs are serialized per document to avoid concurrent conflicting writes; global workers scale horizontally.

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Immediate indexing vs. eventual consistency
  - Choice: Indexing is performed asynchronously after extraction and segmentation. This favors throughput and responsiveness of ingestion endpoints and allows scalable write workloads. However, it introduces eventual consistency for search results; new documents may not appear in search instantly. This design favors maintainability and simplicity for a small team, while still delivering timely search updates. If stronger consistency is required, the system could switch to synchronous indexing with stricter transactional guarantees, at the cost of higher latency and reduced throughput.

- Cons: Additional abstraction layers increase initial development effort and require disciplined interfaces and tests to prevent drift. Early investment in a plugin registry and stable contracts pays off as formats grow.

- Assumptions: Content hash dedup suffices for avoiding duplicates; per-format text extraction quality is acceptable via chosen libraries; eventual consistency in indexing is acceptable for search results.
- Unresolved issues: Selection of concrete storage backends (object store vs. relational), index engine choice (e.g., Lucene-based vs. managed service), and tolerances for partial processing (how partial results affect search visibility).
- Risks: Large PDFs may cause long extraction times; require streaming or chunked processing and potential progress visibility to users.
- Next steps: Validate chosen storage, indexing engine, and format handlers with a small pilot; define concrete SLAs for processing latency and failure alerts.

[Writer]
1. System Overview
The service provides an asynchronous document-processing backend with search capabilities. Users upload PDFs, TXT, and Markdown files. The system validates uploads, stores originals and metadata, extracts text, segments content into searchable units, builds a text index, and supports document search. Processing is asynchronous with retryable steps, idempotent deduplication to prevent duplicate records on repeated uploads, and an extensible format-agnostic architecture to ease adding new formats. Observability, maintainability, and small-team practicality drive the design.

2. Main Components and Responsibilities

- Upload Service
  - Input: raw file, metadata (uploader, timestamp, filename, size, checksum)
  - Output: upload acknowledgment, processing job id, initial validation status
  - Responsibility: validate file type/size, compute content hash, store original file metadata, enqueue processing task; deduplicate by content hash + uploader context.

- Deduplication & Metadata Store
  - Input: file hash, uploader id, filename
  - Output: existing record reference or new record creation
  - Responsibility: prevent duplicate records for repeated uploads; maintain mapping from hash+uploader to document metadata.

- Processing Orchestrator
  - Input: processing job id
  - Output: processing status updates
  - Responsibility: coordinate stages (text extraction, segmentation, indexing) with retry/backoff; maintain idempotent state transitions; orchestrate retries on transient failures.

- Format Handler Registry (Plugin System)
  - Input: document type, raw bytes
  - Output: extracted plain text, extraction metadata
  - Responsibility: provide per-format extraction logic; easy extension to new formats via plug-in handlers.

- Text Extraction Service
  - Input: document blob, format-specific handler
  - Output: extracted text stream or chunks, extraction metadata
  - Responsibility: perform robust text extraction, handle large documents incrementally; expose progressive results for long documents.

- Document Segmentation Service
  - Input: extracted text
  - Output: a sequence of searchable sections (e.g., paragraphs or fixed-size chunks) with section metadata
  - Responsibility: divide text into searchable units suitable for indexing; support configurable granularity.

- Indexing Service
  - Input: sections with metadata, document id
  - Output: index data stored in a search engine (or internal inverted index)
  - Responsibility: build and maintain a searchable index; support incremental indexing on updates.

- Search Service
  - Input: query text, filters, pagination
  - Output: ranked document/section results
  - Responsibility: execute full-text search, apply metadata filters, return results with enough context to display in UI.

- Storage & Metadata Store
  - Input: original file, derived data, index state
  - Output: stored blobs and metadata
  - Responsibility: reliably store originals (object store), extracted text/segments, and index state; provide versioning and immutability where appropriate.

- State & Job Registry
  - Input: job status updates
  - Output: status visibility to clients (via API)
  - Responsibility: track asynchronous workflow state, retries, failure reasons, and completion.

- API Gateway (optional in-design)
  - Input/Output: REST API requests/responses
  - Responsibility: authentication/authorization, rate limiting, request routing to services.

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow

- Upload:
  - Client posts a file and metadata to /api/uploads.
  - Upload Service validates type/size, computes content hash, checks dedup store.
  - If duplicate found (same hash + uploader), return existing document reference; otherwise create a new document record and enqueue a processing job.

- Processing orchestration:
  - Processing Orchestrator picks up the job, transitions to EXTRACTING; invokes the Format Handler Registry to select a handler by format.
  - Text Extraction Service extracts text (potentially streaming), producing extraction metadata and a textual payload.
  - Segmenter divides text into sections; each section is associated with offsets and source document id.
  - Indexing Service ingests sections into the search index; on success, mark document as READY in state store.
  - Any transient failure triggers a retry with exponential backoff; non-recoverable failures transition to FAILED with logs.

- Search:
  - Client queries /api/search with keywords and optional filters.
  - Search Service queries the index, returns ranked results with snippet and document references, including processing status if relevant.

- Status reporting:
  - Clients can poll /api/uploads/{id}/status to observe progress, or use webhooks/events if configured.

4. REST API Design

API Table

- Method: POST
  Endpoint: /api/uploads
  Purpose: Upload a document; start processing if not a duplicate
  Main Request Data: {
    "uploaderId": "user-123",
    "filename": "report.pdf",
    "contentType": "application/pdf",
    "sizeBytes": 248901,
    "content": "<base64-encoded-chunked-content>" // or multi-part form data
  }
  Main Response Data: {
    "documentId": "doc-789",
    "jobId": "job-456",
    "status": "QUEUED",
    "duplicate": false
  }

- Method: GET
  Endpoint: /api/uploads/{documentId}/status
  Purpose: Retrieve processing status
  Main Request Data: none
  Main Response Data: {
    "documentId": "doc-789",
    "status": "PROCESSING",
    "progress": 42,
    "details": "Text extraction in progress"
  }

- Method: GET
  Endpoint: /api/search
  Purpose: Search across indexed documents
  Main Request Data: {
    "query": "annual report",
    "filters": { "uploaderId": "user-123" },
    "page": 1,
    "size": 10
  }
  Main Response Data: {
    "results": [
      {
        "documentId": "doc-789",
        "score": 0.92,
        "snippet": "... key findings ...",
        "metadata": { "filename": "report.pdf", "uploaderId": "user-123" }
      }
    ],
    "total": 1,
    "page": 1
  }

- Method: POST
  Endpoint: /api/docs/{documentId}/retry
  Purpose: Manually trigger a retry of a failed step (admin action)
  Main Request Data: { "step": "text_extraction" }
  Main Response Data: { "status": "RETRY_SCHEDULED" }

5. Data Model

- Document
  - documentId: string
  - uploaderId: string
  - filename: string
  - contentType: string
  - sizeBytes: number
  - hash: string
  - status: string (QUEUED, PROCESSING, EXTRACTING, INDEXING, READY, FAILED)
  - createdAt: timestamp
  - updatedAt: timestamp
  - metadata: JSON

- DocumentVersion / Record
  - versionId: string
  - documentId: string
  - createdAt: timestamp
  - sourceFormat: string
  - extractionNotes: JSON

- Section
  - sectionId: string
  - documentId: string
  - offsetStart: number
  - offsetEnd: number
  - text: string
  - indexVectors: optional

- IndexEntry
  - indexId: string
  - sectionId: string
  - documentId: string
  - term: string
  - position: number
  - weight: number

- ProcessingJob
  - jobId: string
  - documentId: string
  - step: string
  - status: string
  - createdAt: timestamp
  - updatedAt: timestamp
  - retryCount: int
  - lastError: string

- DedupRecord
  - dedupId: string
  - hash: string
  - uploaderId: string
  - documentId: string
  - createdAt: timestamp

6. Suggested Project Structure

- cmd/
  - uploader-service/
  - api-gateway/
- pkg/
  - storage/
  - duplicates/
  - formats/
  - extraction/
  - segmentation/
  - indexing/
  - search/
  - queues/
  - api/
- internal/
  - processors/
  - models/
  - utils/
- config/
- tests/
  - unit/
  - integration/

Key design ideas:
- Separate concerns via micro-service style boundaries, but within a monorepo for maintainability.
- Use a central job/queue (e.g., Redis-backed) to coordinate asynchronous steps with backoff.
- Format handlers registered in a registry; default handlers for PDF, TXT, Markdown; add new handlers by implementing a defined interface and registering them.

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention

- Validation errors (invalid type, oversized file) result in immediate 4xx with actionable messages; no side effects.
- Transient failures (network, IO, temporary unavailability) trigger exponential backoff retries up to a configurable max (e.g., 7 attempts over hours).
- Idempotency keys: on upload, compute content hash; if a matching hash and uploaderId exist, return existing documentId and status to prevent duplicates. If the hash exists but the document is CANCELLED/FAILED, allow re-upload to create a new record while preserving history.
- Retries of specific steps (extraction, indexing) are isolated per job; failures do not cascade to unrelated jobs.
- Partial processing: if extraction succeeds but indexing fails, document remains PROCESSING/INDEXING with a retry path to complete indexing; partial indices can be reconciled via idempotent index updates.

8. Testing Plan

- Unit tests for:
  - Hash dedup logic and metadata association
  - Format registry dispatching to correct handler
  - Segmenter output guarantees (non-empty sections, deterministic boundaries)
  - Retry/backoff calculations
- Integration tests:
  - End-to-end upload -> processing -> search path with sample PDFs, TXT, Markdown
  - Duplicate upload prevention path
  - Failure-mode tests (forcing extraction/indexing failures and ensuring backoff/retry)
- Performance tests (sampling small documents; plan for larger documents as future work)
- Observability tests:
  - Verify status transitions and metrics emission (latency, throughput, error rate)
  - Trace flow across services for a single job

9. Design Tradeoff

- Tradeoff: Centralized queue vs fully distributed orchestrator
  - A centralized, pluggable queue simplifies retries, backoff, and idempotency, reducing the likelihood of duplicate work in a small team setting. It also provides clear checkpoints for observability. However, it adds a single point of contention and potential bottlenecks if throughput scales drastically. To mitigate, the design supports horizontal scaling of the queue system and stateless workers, enabling easier growth and maintainability while preserving deterministic retry semantics.

Evidence of assumptions and unresolved issues to Manager:
- Assumes base64 or multi-part upload capability; actual payload handling will depend on chosen runtime (streaming vs memory-bound ingestion).
- Requires selection of storage backend (object store + relational/NoSQL). Choice of specific technologies (e.g., S3, Postgres, Redis) to be finalized by the Researcher.
- Exact indexing engine (Lucene-based, Elastic-derived, or in-process inverted index) to be chosen; initial plan supports pluggable backends.
- Concurrency controls for concurrent uploads by the same user and how to surface user-visible progress in real-time (potential need for websockets/push events).
- Compliance and retention policies for metadata and documents are pending legal review and may affect storage lifecycle.

Instruction:
Produce the self-contained final deliverable after independently checking the Manager decision against every task constraint and verified worker result.

Return only the content for your role. Do not include hidden chain-of-thought. Use concise reasoning summaries where useful.

final_output:
1) System Overview
Goal: a modular backend that accepts PDFs, TXT, and Markdown, validates and stores originals and metadata, extracts text, chunks into searchable sections, builds an index, and supports document search. Processing is asynchronous with retryable steps, idempotent deduplication by content hash, and a pluggable format-handler registry to ease adding new formats. Non-functional goals include maintainability for a small team, observability (logs/metrics/traces), and eventual consistency for indexing.

2) Main Components and Responsibilities
- API Gateway: validates requests, enforces size/type limits, forwards to ingestion, and exposes status/search endpoints.
- Ingestion Service: computes content hash, checks dedup store, stores original blob and metadata, creates document record, and enqueues processing jobs.
- Deduplication & Metadata Store: maintains hash → document mapping; enforces idempotent ingestion and duplicate prevention.
- Storage Layer: Original Store (blob/object), Metadata/State Store (document records, hashes, statuses).
- Text Extraction Engine (Format Registry): pluggable per-format extractors; extracts plain text and per-format metadata; supports streaming for large files.
- Sectionizer/Chunker: splits extracted text into searchable sections with offsets and metadata.
- Indexing Service: writes/upserts inverted index entries for sections; supports incremental indexing; idempotent replay handling.
- Search Service: executes queries against the index; returns hits with snippets and provenance.
- Processing Job/Queue: orchestrates steps with retries and backoff; per-document idempotent replay ensures safety.
- Status & Observability: exposes status endpoints, emits logs/metrics/traces, and provides retry/error counts.
- Format Handler Registry: central registry to add new formats with minimal core changes; maps formatKey to handler.

3) End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Upload: POST /documents
  - Validate file type and size; compute content hash; check dedup store. If duplicate exists, return existing document_id and status; else store original, create document with status QUEUED, enqueue processing pipeline.
- Extraction: worker selects extractor via registry; extract text (stream if supported); produce per-format metadata.
- Segmentation: Sectionizer splits text into searchable units with start/end offsets.
- Indexing: Indexer ingests sections, updates inverted index; marks document as INDEXED when complete.
- Search: GET /search queries index and returns ranked results with snippets.
- Status reporting: GET /documents/{id}/status reports stage, progress, last_error, retry_count.
- Retries: exponential backoff with jitter; max attempts per step; idempotent writes prevent duplicates on replay.
- Dedup logic: content hash dedup; identical hash means reuse existing document_id; edge cases handled via policy (e.g., versioning if metadata differs).

4) REST API Design
- POST /documents
  - Purpose: upload and start processing
  - Request Data: multipart/form-data with file, filename, size_bytes, content_type, user_id, optional metadata JSON
  - Response Data: { document_id, status, duplicate: false, retry_after_ms? }
- GET /documents/{id}/status
  - Purpose: status inquiry
  - Response Data: { document_id, status, progress, last_error, retry_count, updated_at }
- GET /search
  - Purpose: search index
  - Request Data: query, page, page_size, filters
  - Response Data: { total, page, page_size, results: [{ document_id, score, snippet, metadata }] }
- POST /documents/{id}/retry
  - Purpose: manual retry trigger
  - Response Data: { document_id, status, next_retry_after_ms }

5) Data Model (essential entities)
- Document
  - document_id (UUID)
  - user_id
  - filename
  - content_type
  - size_bytes
  - hash (SHA-256)
  - storage_ref (original blob key)
  - status (QUEUED, EXTRACTING, SEGMENTING, INDEXING, INDEXED, FAILED)
  - created_at, updated_at
  - last_error (nullable)
  - retry_count
- Section
  - section_id (UUID)
  - document_id (FK)
  - start_offset, end_offset
  - text
  - metadata (JSON)
- IndexEntry
  - index_id (UUID)
  - document_id (FK)
  - section_id (FK, nullable)
  - term or token
  - position
  - weight
- ProcessingJob
  - job_id (UUID)
  - document_id (FK)
  - step (extract, segment, index)
  - attempt
  - next_retry_at
  - status (PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, SUCCESS, FAILED)
  - last_error
- DedupRecord
  - dedup_id (UUID)
  - hash
  - user_id
  - document_id
  - created_at
- FormatHandlerRegistry
  - format_key (pdf, txt, md)
  - handler_reference
  - supported_options

6) Suggested Project Structure
- cmd/
  - api_server/
- internal/
  - api/ (DTOs, handlers)
  - ingestion/ (upload, validation)
  - storage/ (blob_store, metadata_store)
  - processing/ (orchestrator)
  - formats/ (registry + pdf_extractor.go, txt_extractor.go, md_extractor.go)
  - segmenter/ (sectionizer)
  - indexing/ (index_writer)
  - search/ (query engine)
  - queue/ (scheduler, backoff)
  - status/ (status computation)
  - registry/ (format registry)
  - config/ (env/config)
- pkg/
  - models/ (Document, Section, IndexEntry, ProcessingJob)
  - errors/ (error codes)
  - utils/ (hashing, validation, backoff)
- tests/
  - unit/
  - integration/
  - end-to-end/
- infra/
  - docker/
  - monitoring/
- docs/
  - design_notes.md
  - runbooks/

7) Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Deduplication: compute content_hash; if hash exists for same uploader and document status is not FAILED, return existing document_id; if FAILED, allow re-upload to create a new document while preserving history.
- Retries: per-step exponential backoff with jitter; max attempts configurable; idempotent processing to avoid duplicates on replay.
- Errors: structured codes (VALIDATION_ERROR, TRANSIENT_ERROR, EXTRACT_ERROR, INDEX_ERROR); surface user-facing details via status; non-sensitive details in logs.
- Duplicate edge cases: metadata-only changes may warrant a new record or a versioned document per policy; default to hash-based dedup to prevent waste.
- Observability: structured logs with document_id, job_id, step, retry_count; metrics for times, throughput, and failures.

8) Testing Plan
- Unit: hash/dedup logic; registry dispatch; sectioner outputs; backoff calculations.
- Integration: end-to-end flow for each format; duplicate path; partial failures.
- End-to-end: ingest → search results with proper snippets and ranking.
- Performance: large file extraction and indexing cadence; concurrent uploads.
- Regression: ensure new formats via registry do not disrupt existing ones.
- Observability: verify logs/metrics/traces are emitted with correct fields.

9) Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: eventual vs strong indexing consistency
  - Chosen: eventual indexing. This yields higher throughput and simpler maintenance for a small team, at the cost of brief indexing latency after ingestion. If strict immediacy is required, a synchronous indexing path could be added, but with greater complexity and potential throughput constraints.

Notes for Implementation
- Format Registry: define a common interface (initialize, extract, segment) and a registry map format_key → handler; new formats registered with minimal core changes.
- Dedup key: SHA-256 on file content; treat duplicates as existing documents when appropriate to prevent wasteful re-ingestion.
- Backplane: a Redis-like queue or similar for at-least-once delivery; workers are idempotent and replay-safe.
- Pseudocode and payloads can be supplied on request to illustrate:
  - Hash computation and idempotent ingestion
  - Registry dispatch example
  - Simple exponential backoff function
- Example payloads (JSON) can be constructed if needed to demonstrate typical API traffic and status responses.

Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After upload, it must validate file type and size, store the original file and metadata, extract text, divide text into searchable sections, create a searchable index, support document search, and report processing status.

The design must also support asynchronous processing, retryable failed steps, prevention of unnecessary duplicate records for repeated uploads, easy addition of new document formats, and maintainability for a small development team.

The response should be approximately 700-900 words. No external citations are required. Do not claim that the proposed system has been implemented, empirically tested, or proven to achieve specific performance results.
